messageText: Cynda Media Lab is an award-winning transdisciplinary design studio.
We specialize in branding, motion graphics, app/web design, and experience design.
Visit our website: http://cyndamedialab.com
I have been working with Cynda Media Lab for the past 6 years.
Since 2019. I have been a developer for the studio and have worked on a variety of projects.
This is not a full-time position, for me personally is like a Freelance Agency and I am happy to be part of the team.
During the global pandemic, MIT and FIT couldnt hold their textile workshop in person, so they decided to create a virtual version of the workshop.
As part of a small team we developed a custom educational tool for the collaboration class
between MIT and FIT. You can learn more about the project here: https://cyndamedia.com/index.php/portfolio/mit-and-fit-remote-learning-hub/
The stack includes a restful API with Express and MongoDB, React with Typescript,
Jest for unit testing and ESLint for linting.
Tasks I was responsible for included:
- Developed atomic / reusable UI components using storybook for quick prototyping and testing.
- Developed mobile friendly pages by assembling responsive UI components.
- Integrated API endpoints on both view and edit pages, providing a custom experience for logged in teachers and students.
- Updates to the student portal for the upcoming semester to include additional edit pages and teacher workflows.
Here are some of the components I worked on:
Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) and Fashion Institute of Technology (FIT) joined forces to create an innovative textile workshop
They had a reasearch collaboration for multiple years for Advanced Functional Fabrics of America (AFFOA).

FIT and MIT teamed up for a workshop, held collaboratively with AFFOA, where they would have students from both institutes spent some time together and collaborate and learn about the project and the fabrications.
This is a WordPress website that was already built prior to me joining Cynda.
At first I was mostly just managing the content but it evolved to:
- Creating and designing new pages
- Adding custom CSS to style elements as specified by the client
- Integrating WordPress plugins to restrict access to certain pages and content
- Creating custom application forms
There were multiple times where adjusments or updates to some of the content were needed for the past few years.
There was a complete redesign of the website in 2022 that allowed for more freedom to design and build the website as we wanted.
The revamp of the WordPress site, MITandFiT.info, it was made with Gatsby and WordPress as the CMS. This allowed for less limitations in the styling of the website.
- Responsible for content management in WordPress.
- Created a Components Library with React and Storybooks.
- Made custom WP Blocks with JSX that called the Components Library to use as preview when adding blocks in the CMS.
This is a WordPress microsite is made out of custom blocks.
- Responsible for content management in WordPress.
- Created responsive blocks with JSX that are styled for both the Editor and Front-end.
- Made a custom theme in order to keep the navigation bar and footer consistent through all the pages.
This is a WordPress site that helps connect students with design educators. https://designportfolio.center/
- Developed UI components using lit-html and Storybook for quick prototyping and testing.
- Created custom WordPress blocks with JSX that are styled for both the live preview editor and published website.
The colorful font design has a custom color palette that is constantly changing.
Moreover, all the components in this page support a dark mode to make it more responsitve to the users preferences.
I migrated some static pages to Webflow, rebuilding existing pages and developing new ones from provided designs and integrating Webflow CMS to enable dynamic content management.

Built an AI-powered search page that uses the full website as a knowledge base to deliver accurate, context-aware responses. The AI agent was developed and deployed on Vercel and embedded directly into the Webflow search page.
Visit the live website at https://www.fabric.vc/
The pages are responsive and has many animations and hover effects to make the user experience more engaging and interactive.
I built an AI-powered search page that uses the full website as a knowledge base to deliver accurate, context-aware responses.
The AI agent was developed and deployed on Vercel and embedded directly into the Webflow search page.
